title: RNA Isolation from Plant Tissue Protocol 12: Hot Acid Phenol Method for Angiosperms

steps_raw.instruction: Add 3 mL of saturated acid phenol (pH 4.3) and 3 mL of RNA extraction buffer to a 15 ml snap cap tube. Warm tube to 65 °C in a fume hood using a heat block.
Homogenize tissue in liquid nitrogen using a mortar and pestle.

Transfer up to 1.0 g of ground tissue to a tube containing the phenol-buffer mixture and close the snap cap tube completely (second stop). Immediately mix tube by hand. Vortex until the phases mix and appear cloudy. Keep at Room temperature or 65 °C depending on whether SDS is precipitating. Use a spatula and funnel chilled in liquid nitrogen to transfer the powdered tissue to the tube.
Centrifuge at for 00:10:00 .
Transfer aqueous phase to a new 15 ml tube.
Add 3 mL of chloroform:isoamyl (24:1 ratio). Close the snap cap tube completely (second stop) and mix contents immediately by hand. Then vortex until the phases mix and appear cloudy.
Centrifuge at for 00:10:00 .
Transfer aqueous phase to a new 15 ml tube.
Precipitate nucleic acids by gently adding 3 mL of isopropanol to the tube followed by gentle inversion to mix the phases. Precipitate for 01:00:00 at 4 °C .
Centrifuge at for 00:10:00 .
Remove the supernatant without dislodging the pellet.
Rinse the pellet with 1 mL of chilled 70 % ethanol made with RNase-free H 2 O.
Transfer pellet and ethanol to a new 1.5 ml tube and invert to wash.
Centrifuge at in a microcentrifuge for 00:02:00 and remove the supernatant.

Spin dry the RNA pellet by successive removal of any remaining liquid following 00:01:00 centrifugations at maximum speed in a microcentrifuge. Repeat this process until no liquid is seen when the tube is flicked. Allow the pellet to air dry for 00:01:00 to 00:05:00 , depending on the size of the pellet. Avoid over-drying since this will negatively affect re-suspension in water.
While on ice, re-suspend the pellet in 500 µL RNase-free H 2 O using gentle pipetting. Ensure the pellet is completely re-suspended before proceeding to step 17.
Add 500 µL of 4 Molarity (M) LiCl. Precipitate the RNA overnight at 4 °C .
Centrifuge at for 00:30:00 at 4 °C in a microcentrifuge.

Wash the pellet three times with 200 µL of chilled 70 % ethanol diluted in RNase-free H 2 O. Centrifuge at 4 °C . Remove the pellet from the wall of the tube to wash the pellet more completely. Pellet will now dislodge easily and care must be taken when removing the supernatant between washes.
Spin the pellet. Remove ethanol and dry as in step 15. go to step #15 Centrifuge at 4 °C .

Dissolve pellet in RNase-free H 2 O on ice. The volume of the water is dependent on the size and clarity of the pellet, as well as the viscosity of the re-suspension, but 50 µL can be used as a standard starting point.
Centrifuge RNA extract for 00:01:00 at 4 °C . Check concentration and integrity using a NanoDrop 2000 spectrophotometer.

Purify and DNase treat up to 100 µg of RNA on a RNeasy Mini Kit spin column (pink) by Qiagen. Follow the ‘RNA Cleanup’ protocol as published in the RNeasy Handbook , including the on-column DNase digestion with the RNase-Free DNase Set by Qiagen.
Elute RNA twice from the column using 40 µL of 95 °C RNase-free water to a total of 80 µL . However, if the mass of RNA applied to the column is less than 50 µg , then use only 40 µL to elute.
Add 3 mL of saturated acid phenol (pH 4.3) and 3 mL of RNA extraction buffer to a 15 ml snap cap tube.
Transfer up to 1.0 g of ground tissue to a tube containing the phenol-buffer mixture and close the snap cap tube completely (second stop).
Vortex until the phases mix and appear cloudy.
Keep at Room temperature or 65 °C depending on whether SDS is precipitating.
Add 3 mL of chloroform:isoamyl (24:1 ratio).
Close the snap cap tube completely (second stop) and mix contents immediately by hand.
Precipitate nucleic acids by gently adding 3 mL of isopropanol to the tube followed by gentle inversion to mix the phases. 01:00:00 at 4 °C .
Spin dry the RNA pellet by successive removal of any remaining liquid following 00:01:00 centrifugations at maximum speed in a microcentrifuge.
Allow the pellet to air dry for 00:01:00 to 00:05:00 , depending on the size of the pellet. Avoid over-drying since this will negatively affect re-suspension in water.
Add 500 µL of 4 Molarity (M) LiCl. 4 °C .
Spin the pellet.
Centrifuge at 4 °C .
Centrifuge RNA extract for 00:01:00 at 4 °C .
Warm tube to 65 °C in a fume hood using a heat block.
Immediately mix tube by hand.
Then vortex until the phases mix and appear cloudy.
Precipitate for 01:00:00 at 4 °C .
Repeat this process until no liquid is seen when the tube is flicked.
Precipitate the RNA overnight at 4 °C .
Remove supernatant by pipette.
Remove ethanol and dry as in step 15. go to step #15
Check concentration and integrity using a NanoDrop 2000 spectrophotometer.
